title: Fixed water spray for Class A petroleum tanks
requirement: Class 'A' Petroleum in above ground tanks (floating or fixed roof) shall have fixed water spray system, having capacity larger than 190 m3 or diameter greater than 6 m.

title: Fire water header pressure
requirement: The fire water system shall be designed for a minimum pressure of 7.0 kg/cm2 at the hydraulically remotest point of application at grade level and minimum residual pressure of 5.25 kg/cm2 at landing valve at elevated structure on designed flow rate at that point.

title: Jockey pump capacity and auto cut-in
requirement: The capacity of jockey pumps shall be minimum 1% of the design fire water rate. Its head shall be higher than the main fire water pumps. Auto cut-in / cut-off facility should be provided for jockey pumps.

title: Annual visual external inspection of storage tanks
requirement: Visual external inspection of each tank shall be made once a year.

title: Tank mountings checked yearly after monsoon
requirement: Tank mountings such as Breather Valves, P&V Valves, Relief Valves, Flame arrestors shall be ensured clean and operable every year after monsoon.

title: Relief valve test periodicity
requirement: Safety relief valves in existing units shall be inspected and tested at every turnaround but not later than 4 years (non-critical services; the 2012 revision allows up to 5 years based on operating history). First test within one to three years of commissioning depending on service.
note: Interval is revision-dependent (4 y in earlier text, up to 5 y in 2012 rev). PSV-1101 last test 2022-04-12 breaches either reading only in 2026/2027; the plant's own deferral email (Nov 2024) already flags it overdue against its internal window.

title: Gas test prerequisites for hot work / vessel entry
requirement: Gas test for hydrocarbon, oxygen deficiency and toxic gases shall be conducted as a pre-requisite to issue of permit for hot work or vessel entry; no hot work unless explosive-meter reading is zero. For confined space entry oxygen must be 19.5-23.5%; vessel entry without hot work may be permitted with combustible gas up to 5% LEL.

title: Permit validity and revalidation
requirement: Permit valid for the shift unless renewed; may be revalidated shift-wise for not more than seven calendar days. Personnel authorized to issue or receive permits shall be trained for not less than one day on the permit system.

title: Pressure plant - safe working pressure
requirement: If in any factory, any plant or machinery or any part thereof is operated at a pressure above atmospheric pressure, effective measures shall be taken to ensure that the safe working pressure of such plant or machinery or part is not exceeded.

title: Precautions against dangerous fumes - confined spaces
requirement: No person shall be required or allowed to enter any confined space in which any gas, fume, vapour or dust is likely to be present so as to involve risk, until all practicable measures have been taken to remove it and unless (a) a certificate in writing has been given by a competent person, based on a test carried out by himself, that the space is reasonably free from dangerous gas, fume, vapour or dust; or (b) the person wears suitable breathing apparatus and a safety belt/rope held by a person outside.

title: Portable electric light in confined spaces
requirement: No portable electric light or other electric appliance of voltage exceeding 24 volts shall be permitted inside any confined space unless adequate safety devices are provided; where flammable gas may be present only flame-proof lamps are permitted.

title: Explosive or inflammable dust, gas
requirement: Where any manufacturing process produces dust, gas, fume or vapour likely to explode on ignition, all practicable measures shall be taken to prevent such explosion by effective enclosure of plant, removal or prevention of accumulation, and exclusion or effective enclosure of possible sources of ignition.

title: Precautions in case of fire - escape, equipment, training
requirement: In every factory, all practicable measures shall be taken to prevent outbreak of fire and its spread, and to provide and maintain safe means of escape and the necessary equipment and facilities for extinguishing fire. Effective measures shall be taken to ensure all workers are familiar with the means of escape and have been adequately trained in the routine to be followed (basis for periodic fire/emergency drills; State Factories Rules and OISD practice for MAH units expect at least quarterly drills).

title: Safety of buildings and machinery
requirement: If it appears to the Inspector that any building, ways, machinery or plant is in such condition that it is dangerous to human life or safety, he may order measures in writing; where danger is imminent he may prohibit its use until properly repaired or altered.

title: Five-year hydraulic test of pressure vessels
requirement: All vessels shall be hydraulically tested by a competent person at a pressure marked on the vessel at intervals of not more than five years after the date of first test; for vessels containing corrosive or toxic gases the periodic test shall be done at an interval of two years. Internal inspection along with residual thickness measurement shall be carried out during the periodic testing.

title: Annual relief valve test on pressure vessels
requirement: Relief valves shall be tested by a Competent Person for correct operation at least once in a year and a record of such test shall be maintained. The test certificate shall be issued in the prescribed proforma.

title: Licence required for petroleum storage
requirement: No person shall store petroleum except under and in accordance with a licence granted under these rules (limited exceptions for well-head tanks and transit cargo).

title: Tank earthing and annual earth-resistance test
requirement: Every tank for storage of petroleum in bulk shall be electrically connected with the earth by not less than two separate and distinct connections at opposite extremities; resistance to earth shall not exceed 7 ohms. The connections shall be inspected and tested by a competent person at least once in every twelve months and a record of such tests maintained.
